LAW AND MOTION 302, COURT ISSUES WRITTEN ORDER SUSTAINING DEMURRER WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. MATTER PREVIOUSLY TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION BY JUDGE CURTIS KARNOW ON JUL-23-2012.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION. (SEE CLERK'S MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS OF RULING.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.
MINI MINUTES FOR APR-27-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEMURRER SUBMITTED ON JUL-23-2012 BY CURTIS KARNOW. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT VACATED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD AND COMPLAINT FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF WAS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: CURTIS E.A. KARNOW; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976

calendar.matters:
DEMURRER
Off Calendar Jul-24-2012 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Rafal Ofierski, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.4244, appearing for Defendant and moving party;
Kenneth Absalom, Esq. of the Law Offices of Kenneth Absalom, Tel. 415.392.5040, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party.

The matter was argued. The court vacated its tentative ruling and DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD AND COMPLAINT FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF was taken under submission. Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976

Notice Of Demurrer To Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award; Memo P/A
Off Calendar Apr-27-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD.
Appearances: David A. Delbon of the Office of the City Attorney (554.3962) for respondent and moving party; no other appearances.
The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to both causes of action. As currently pled, neither cause of action alleges a cognizable claim, although the second cause of action is not necessarily and clearly time-barred. As to the first cause of action, Petitioner is granted leave to amend, if it can do so in good faith, to allege which mandatory "strictures" the Board failed to follow. As to the second cause of action, Petitioner is granted leave to amend, if it can do so in good faith, to allege ultimate facts supporting a basis to vacate the arbitration award. Order signed.
(302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.

Notice Of Demurrer To Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award; Memo P/A
